purpose: Provide Gemini with a safe, implementation-oriented JSON handoff for a frontend-heavy refactor while preserving the current Android/Compose framework, data flow, and verified behaviors.

high_level_goal.primary: Refactor and improve frontend quality and visual consistency without breaking existing article, config, diary, settings, and GitHub-based workflows.
high_level_goal.secondary: Unify top bars, spacing, hierarchy, and interaction quality across first-level and nested pages.
high_level_goal.non_goal: Do not redesign the backend/data layer architecture from scratch. Do not replace Room/DataStore/GitHub repositories with a new persistence model.

notes: Bottom tabs are Posts, Diary, Config, Settings.
Remote article editing now routes to the same full editor UI as local drafts.
Dependencies are manually wired in AppContainer.
Frontend refactor should prefer reusing existing repositories/viewmodels rather than adding a DI framework.
This is the current shared scaffold for first-level pages.
If top bar consistency is refactored, this is a key file.
Keep all first-level screens aligned to the top with consistent header height and padding.

secondary_routes.name: 结构化远端文件编辑
notes: This route is still needed for config details and diary-related structured editing.
Do not redirect remote articles back to this route.

legacy_or_not_primary.name: PostsRoute
legacy_or_not_primary.status: Legacy / not the main routed article screen
legacy_or_not_primary.warning: Do not spend main refactor effort here unless you are cleaning up compatibility.

article_screen: The article tab must provide a UI switch between local drafts and remote articles.
Inside remote articles, there must be a second UI switch between published remote articles and remote drafts.
Article cards should display title and description from markdown frontmatter metadata, not raw file path as the main label.
Local drafts must remain editable locally and must not be pushed until the user explicitly publishes/uploads.
Remote articles are loaded from GitHub, not from local initial data.
article_editing: Local draft editing and remote article editing must use the same full editor UI.
Remote article editing must not use the old structured remote file editor route.
Remote editor must load markdown/frontmatter from GitHub and map it into the same draft editing surface.
Remote publish must overwrite the exact remote article path, not create a new unrelated article path.
publish_delete_rules: When a local draft is published successfully, the local draft record must be deleted and local asset cache must be deleted.
After local publish success, the user should continue editing that article through the remote article flow, not the deleted local draft.
Remote article delete must delete the GitHub remote article path directly.
Article cards support right-swipe quick delete with second confirmation.
config_center: Config first-level hub must expose two second-level entries: config file and config details.
Config file edits work against GitHub config.ts content.
Config details represent feature/page-specific editable data driven by the Mizuki documentation model.
diary: Diary remains a first-level tab separate from articles.
Diary is complementary to articles and should not be merged into the article tab UI.
github_positioning: GitHub is a core dependency and does not need to be hidden.
The UI can openly explain that remote content comes from GitHub.

notes: Contains owner/repo/branch/update branch/PAT and default article metadata values.

safe_to_change: Composables, layout structure, spacing system, top bar structure, cards, chips, segmented controls, empty states, list item visuals, icons, typography hierarchy, content grouping.
Visual polish, animation, component extraction, and route-level layout composition.
Navigation presentation as long as route patterns and destination behavior remain compatible.
change_with_care: ViewModel public state shape if all call sites are updated and tests remain green.
EditorRoute interaction layout; keep behavior and metadata coverage intact.
Config and settings screens; keep all existing fields and educational links available.
avoid_or_only_change_if_required: Any code that changes article publish semantics.

language_and_copy: All visible user-facing copy should be Chinese unless a field is inherently code/config syntax.
Do not leave mixed English placeholder text in end-user UI.
layout_consistency: First-level pages and nested pages should align to the top edge consistently.
Top bars and screen header height should feel unified across tabs and nested routes.
Avoid one screen using a visibly taller header or inconsistent top padding unless there is a strong reason.
interaction_consistency: Loading, empty, success, and error states should all be explicit.
Remote-vs-local origin should always be visually clear.
frontend_quality: Prefer reusable UI patterns over repeated ad hoc cards.
Avoid making the app visually generic, but preserve maintainability.
The refactor may be visually stronger, but must not hide important repository path/status/context from power users.

content_route: Current article screen already includes source switching and remote draft filtering.
Current implementation uses SwipeToDismissBox for swipe delete.
Remote cards open the full editor via editor-remote route.
editor_route: One editor UI handles both local and remote sessions.
Remote session delete dialog should delete remote content directly, not ask about local delete flags.
Inline image insertion and cover import still depend on AssetStorage.
settings_route: Contains educational PAT guidance and external links.
Do not remove GitHub setup instructions unless replacing them with a better Chinese UX.
config_hub: Must remain a two-entry hub rather than flattening everything into one long list.

order: Stabilize shared page shell and top bar system.
Refactor article list screen and cards while preserving current filters and delete actions.
Refactor full editor layout and metadata sections without removing fields.
Refactor config hub/config detail/config file visual consistency.
Refactor settings visual hierarchy and instructional blocks.
Polish diary and any remaining nested pages for consistency.
technical_style: Prefer extracting small presentational composables over giant route functions.
Reuse existing viewmodels unless a measured simplification is clearly better.

must_pass: Article tab still supports local draft vs remote article switching.
Remote article tab still supports published vs remote draft switching.
Remote articles still open in the full editor.
Local publish still deletes the local draft on success.
Right-swipe delete still works with confirmation for both local and remote article cards.
Config tab still exposes config file and config details as separate second-level entries.
Diary remains separate from article flow.
Settings still expose GitHub repo/branch/PAT/update-branch fields and guidance.
visual: Top bars and top spacing look unified across first-level and nested routes.
Important metadata and status are readable without opening each item.

important: The repository currently contains uncommitted local modifications related to the new remote article workflow.
Gemini should not discard current worktree changes unless the user explicitly asks for a reset.
Continue working on the update branch, not main.

explicit_warnings: Do not regress remote article editing back to RepositoryFileRoute.
Do not reintroduce local-only initial fake data for article/config content.
Do not make publish actions auto-push while typing; push must still happen only on explicit publish/upload.
Do not delete GitHub instructional content from settings without replacing it with an equally clear Chinese explanation.
